item.title: "Still Life Arrangement & Painting" Mondays, April 6th and 13th, 6pm-9pm #04062602 - 04/06/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/13/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by March 30th. Paint your own still life with your favorite item and fruit or flowers. You will paint in water-mixable oils with Carol Hoffman on two Mondays April 6th and 13th from 6pm-9pm. These are 3 hour classes. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the canvas are included in the price of the class. Cost is $70 for TAG members & $80 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419-547-8610, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: "Barn on the Hill" Thursday, April 9, 2026 10am-1pm or 6pm-9pm #04092601 or 04092602 - 04/09/2026 10:00am EDT to 04/09/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by April 9. Paint "Barn on Hill" in water-mixable oils with Carol Hoffman on Thursday, April 9 from 10am until 1 pm or from 6pm-9pm. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the 9"x12" canvas are included in the price of the class. Please bring a large box to take your painting home in. Cost is $50 for TAG members & $60 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419-547-8610, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: Seneca County: A Land of Immigrants - 04/09/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/09/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin-Seneca Public Library invites you to "Seneca County: A Land of Immigrants" on Thursday, April 9 at 6:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Join Local History and Genealogy Specialist Jessica Hoehn for an engaging exploration of the rich cultural heritage of Seneca County. This program will delve into the stories of various immigrant groups who have settled in the area, highlighting their challenges and the lasting impact on community traditions, industries, and neighborhoods. The event is free and open to all adults and is part of T-SPL’s America 250 celebration. For more information, call (419) 447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Game Bird Workshop - 04/09/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/09/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 100 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Are you interested in raising pheasants, quail, or other game birds for sport, meat production, or conservation? This comprehensive workshop will provide you with the essential knowledge and practical skills needed to start and manage your own healthy flock. Whether you're a small-scale farmer, a future hunting preserve owner, or a hobbyist, join us to learn the best practices for successful game bird husbandry. This workshop is free but registration is required. Dinner will be provided starting at 5:30pm.
item.dc:creator: Ohio State University Extension - Seneca County

item.title: Downtown Trivia Night at the Tiffin Eagles - 04/09/2026 7:00pm EDT to 04/09/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Groups of all ages are invited to compete in two rounds of general trivia questions plus the final questions of local interest. Answers will be judged by local celebrities. Participants are asked to form teams of 6 or fewer, which can be composed of all ages. Teams will write their answers to be judged by local celebrities. Questions will be geared toward ages 12 and up. No phones, tablets, or electronic devices may be accessed during the contest. A traveling trophy will be awarded to the winning team. **ONE GrowthZone ticket per team. List each of the team members on the ticket.

item.description: Location : 89 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us on April 10th from 6 PM to 7:30 PM to learn how to create homemade dog treats and nutritious meals for your furry friend. item.title: Marseilles Recycling Drop Off collection weekend - 04/11/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/11/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA You can drop off your recyclables every month on the 2nd weekend from Friday 4pm through Monday 8am here. We will take aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard and books. We ask that shredded paper be bagged. We also ask that your food and drink containers are rinsed clean. You can leave the caps on your items. https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2417
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center

item.title: "White Trees" Saturday, April 11, 2026 9am-1pm #04112601 - 04/11/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/11/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by April 4th. Paint "White Trees" in acrylic with Carol Hoffman on Saturday, April 11 from 9 am until 1 pm. This is a 4 hour class. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the 12x16" canvas are included in the price of the class. Please bring a large box to take your painting home in. Cost is $55 for TAG members & $65 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419-547-8610, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.

item.title: 4th Annual F.A.C.T. Pound Out Cancer - 04/11/2026 10:00am EDT to 04/11/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA This is the 4th year for this event! We will have vendors and a kick butt silent auction! And don't forget POUND! What is POUND? Cardio drumming on steroids! BUT, it is for everybody and every BODY. From 4 years old to 100 years old, everyone can do it!! Buy a shirt, join in the Pound, check out the vendors and food trucks. Make a donation to F.A.C.T! For more info contact us via messenger or Jen at 419-618-4066

item.title: 2026 Downtown Tiffin Chocolate Walk - 04/11/2026 11:00am EDT to 04/11/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 112 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Downtown Tiffin Chocolate Walk Treat yourself to a sweet afternoon in downtown Tiffin at the Downtown Tiffin Chocolate Walk on Saturday, April 11, 2026 11:00AM-3:00PM Stroll from business to business and enjoy chocolate treats and samples at 20 participating downtown locations. This self-guided walk is a fun way to shop local, explore downtown, and enjoy a spring day filled with sweet stops along the way. Date: Saturday, April 11, 2026 Time: 11:00AM-3:00PM Location: Downtown Tiffin Tickets: $20 Tickets are limited and must be purchased in advance at: https://tinyurl.com/3aurxrm9 Gather your friends and enjoy a delicious stroll through Downtown Tiffin. Participating businesses are: Bailiwicks Coffee Company Soul Boutique Tiffin Art Guild SSG Ink The Chandelier Community Event Center The Poignon project The Empire Bistro The Social Cigar Reclaim it! item.title: Poker Night at the Fire Station! - 04/11/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/12/2026 12:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 120 Catherine St, Green Springs, OH 44836, USA Saturday Nov 8th Proceeds benefit the Independence day celebration! Doors open: 5pm cards play: 6pm No late entries! $30 buy in unlimited buy backs for first 3 rounds $10 buy-up for after 3rd round blinds double every 30 minutes bring a new player, you and them get an additional $100 in chips Starting stack $1k beer, pop, food will be available for purchase Pre-register and pay by 11/5 for an additional $100 in chips no late registrations after 6pm must be 18 call/ text 419-307-3675 to register Paypal and Square app Available for online payment through the fire department accounts. Only pay through posts from our page and NO OTHERS!
item.dc:creator: Village of Green Springs Independence Day Celebration Committee

item.title: Night of Worship with Rekindle - 04/12/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/12/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 8245 Main St, Old Fort, OH 44861, USA Sunday, April 12, at 6:30 pm, Old Fort Church will host the group Rekindle from Cedarville University for a Night of Worship. This event will be FREE and open to the public. Rekindle is an all-female worship ministry led by young ladies studying worship and they serve the body of Christ primarily at regional women's events, in local churches and on international mission trips. Rekindle represents the worship program of the Department of Music and Worship at Cedarville University. Rekindle's worship services are scripture based, musically excellent, and infused with congregational songs for the Church. Rekindle is a verb meaning "relight (a fire)" or to "revive (something that has been lost)." Just as David cried out for God to "revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you (Psalm 85:6)," so too Rekindle seeks to lead the people of God to cry out for that revival spirit to return to the Church.
item.dc:creator: Old Fort Church

item.title: Seneca County Human Resources Association | April Meeting - 04/14/2026 11:45am EDT to 04/14/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 45 St Lawrence Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca County Human Resources Association for our April meeting on Tuesday, April 14, 2026, from 11:45 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. in the McMullen Conference Room at Mercy Health – Tiffin Hospital. We will be joined by Renee Smith and Sarah Stephens Krupp of Thrive Community Partners. In this session, Thrive Community Partners will share real examples from local employers who identified specific, on-demand needs and how targeted training helped address them. Participants will walk through a simple “on-demand training loop” they can adapt inside their own organizations to quickly respond to emerging workforce challenges. The Seneca County Human Resources Association (SCHRA) connects HR professionals, business leaders, and organizational managers across Seneca County to share best practices, discuss workforce trends, and grow professionally. Meetings focus on practical learning, peer discussion, and updates on programs and resources that support hiring, engagement, compliance, and organizational success. Membership in the Seneca County Human Resources Association (SCHRA) is open to anyone with HR responsibilities or an interest in workforce and personnel development. SCHRA operates on a one-time yearly registration, allowing members to sign up once for the entire year rather than registering for each individual meeting. Registered members receive meeting notices, speaker details, and calendar invitations for every upcoming session throughout the year. To register for SCHRA visit: https://www.developsenecacounty.org/schra-interest-form/
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: T-SPL Talks: Treatment Foster Care - 04/14/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/14/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join Tiffin-Seneca Public Library on Tuesday, April 14 at 6:00 p.m. for "T-SPL Talks: Treatment Foster Care," part of the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read. This event, held in the Frost Kalnow Room, features a presentation from Northwest Ohio Treatment Foster Care, one of this year's Community Impact Partners. Attendees will learn about treatment foster care, its role in supporting children with higher needs, the requirements to become a foster caregiver, and how the community can get involved. The Community Read centers around the book "The Astral Library" by Kate Quinn, promoting unity through reading and discussions. In addition, best-selling author Kate Quinn will speak at a free community event on Tuesday, April 28 at 6:30 p.m. at the Chandelier Community Event Center, followed by a book signing. For more information about the Community Read and upcoming programs, visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.

item.title: Freedom Rising XII - 04/16/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/16/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA ANNOUNCEMENT: FREEDOM RISING XII We are thrilled to officially announce Freedom Rising XII, our annual celebration and largest fundraiser of the year benefiting Sisters in Shelter and it’s an evening you won’t want to miss. This powerful night brings our community together in support of survivors of human trafficking through an unforgettable program that includes: Inspiring program & mission updates Raffle & Silent Auction (with over 100 incredible items last year!) Heavy hors d’oeuvres Cash bar Keynote Speaker: Brittany Joy Figlar Brittany’s story is one of courage, resilience, and transformation. Each time she speaks, she moves hearts and challenges us to see what’s possible when survivors are empowered to rise from victim to victor. Get Involved: • Interested in donating a raffle or silent auction item or basket? We would be deeply grateful for your support. • Sponsorship opportunities are available at multiple levels, offering meaningful ways to stand with survivors while showing your commitment to our mission. $1,000 Gold Sponsorship includes: 1 VIP Table (8 seats) 8 Tickets to event 8 Free drink tickets 2 Sisters in Shelter T-Shirts Swag bag Advertising on Social Media, Newspaper, Printed Materials, and Radio! $500 Silver Sponsorship includes: 4 Tickets to event 4 Free drink tickets Swag bag Advertising on Social Media, Newspaper, Printed Materials, and Radio! • And even if sponsorship isn’t the right fit, every donation, at any amount, makes a difference. We truly could not do this without our community. We are so blessed to live in a place that shows up for local businesses, for organizations, and most importantly… for people. Tickets are now available (https://www.zeffy.com/en-US/ticketing/freedom-rising-xii ) join us for a night of purpose, impact, and hope. This is more than an event. It’s a movement. We look forward to connecting with you: Sisters in Shelter Any Questions or Contact information: 201 S. Washington Street, PO Box 384 Tiffin, Ohio 44883 (567) 205-8771 sistersinshelter@gmail.com
item.dc:creator: Sisters in Shelter

item.title: Freedom Rising XII Celebration and Fundraiser - 04/16/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/16/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Join community and business leaders for an unforgettable evening with Sisters in Shelter at their Freedom Rising XII, 5:00-7:30 pm Thursday April 16, 2026, at The Chandelier Community Event Center, 151 S. Washington Street, Tiffin.

 

The night will include a keynote presentation from Brittany Joy Figlar who brings a voice to the stage that is both courageous and unforgettable. A survivor of human trafficking, Brittany has transformed her lived experience into powerful advocacy—educating communities, training professionals, and challenging systems to better protect and support survivors. Her story is raw, honest, and deeply moving, reminding us not only of the realities of exploitation, but of the strength and resilience it takes to rise beyond it. Hearing Brittany speak is not just inspiring, it’s a moment that stays with you long after the night ends.

 

Your support at this event will directly contribute to critical support services provided for survivors which include shelter, counseling, legal aid, and vocational training; and to expand awareness campaigns by educating community members to recognize the signs of human trafficking and how to report it.

Tickets for the Freedom Rising XII Celebration and Fundraiser are available now.  There will be a cash bar & heavy hors d'oeuvres.  There will be 50/50, raffles, and silent auction with all proceeds to benefit Sisters in Shelter. Business attire is appropriate. Don't miss this opportunity to be a part of something truly impactful. Together we can make a difference.

 

Sisters in Shelter, which began in 2005, is a non-profit organization that supports survivors and illuminate the atrocities of human trafficking through community education and advocacy. Sisters in Shelter provides critical first needs for women survivors of human trafficking: emergency safe housing, medical care, safety planning, and legal advocacy. To donate directly to Sisters in Shelter, or to learn about volunteer opportunities, visit sistersinshelter.com or visit Facebook: Sisters in Shelter.
item.dc:creator: Seneca County Commission on Aging and Sisters in Shelter

item.title: "The Abstract Show" will be displayed from April 1 -25. Intake will be from March 25-28. - 04/16/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 8:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Get ready for a bold, vibrant, and unpredictable art experience! The Abstract Art Exhibit is all about letting go, embracing creativity, and seeing the world through an entirely new lens. Whether you're an artist or an admirer, this is a show that will challenge, inspire, and excite both the creator and the viewer! Abstract art is more than just shapes and colors, it's about emotion, movement, and limitless expression. Every piece in this exhibit pushes the boundaries of imagination, drawing you into a world where rules are meant to be broken, and where creativity knows no limits. Expect a dazzling mix of mediums, including photography, painting, sculpture, textiles, digital art, jewelry, pastels, glass, and more. If you can dream it, it belongs here! Whether youÕre showcasing your work or simply soaking in the creativity, donÕt miss this one-of-a kind exhibit. Step inside, explore, and let your imagination run wild!
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: Into the Woods - 04/16/2026 7:00pm EDT to 04/16/2026 9:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : University Hall, 310 E Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre presents Into the Woods.

Be careful what you wish for... 🌲✨

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre invites you to a spellbinding production of Stephen Sondheim and James Lapine’s Into the Woods. Watch as the worlds of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, and Jack and the Beanstalk collide in a darkly comic exploration of what happens after "happily ever after."

Join a baker and his wife on their quest to break a witch's curse—and discover the true cost of getting everything you ever wanted.

📍 Where: Gundlach Theatre 

📅 When: * April 16, 17, & 18 | 7:00 PM

April 19 | 2:00 PM

Tickets:

 https://www.eventbrite.com/cc/into-the-woods-4810285

Poster Design by Bri Clark, BA’23 and MBA’24

Into the Woods is presented through special arrangement with Music Theatre International (MTI).

 All authorized performance materials are also supplied by MTI.

www.mtishows.com

item.title: Cold Case Analysis and Community Impact: Inside TU’s Fellowship Program - 04/17/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/17/2026 1:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 155 Miami St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The upcoming event features a two-part discussion focusing on significant historical and social themes. The first segment, led by Dr. Michael Curtis, the Director of Tiffin University's Cold Case Fellowship, will analyze the impact of cold case investigations on communities, highlighting the experiences of students involved in the fellowship program. The second segment will be conducted by Dr. Susan Trollinger, a Professor of English and Rhetoric at the University of Dayton, who will explore the embodied rhetoric employed by women during the suffrage movement, examining the ways in which their attire and presentation influenced public perception and mobilization for women's rights. Together, these presentations will offer insights into the intersection of academic inquiry, community engagement, and historical advocacy.
item.dc:creator: LIFE@TU

item.description:
Location : University Hall, 310 E Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre presents Into the Woods.

Be careful what you wish for... 🌲✨

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre invites you to a spellbinding production of Stephen Sondheim and James Lapine’s Into the Woods. Watch as the worlds of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, and Jack and the Beanstalk collide in a darkly comic exploration of what happens after "happily ever after."

Join a baker and his wife on their quest to break a witch's curse—and discover the true cost of getting everything you ever wanted.

📍 Where: Gundlach Theatre 

📅 When: * April 16, 17, & 18 | 7:00 PM

item.title: Nevada Drop Off Recycling Weekend - 04/18/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/18/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA You can drop off your recyclables every month on the 3rd weekend of the month from Friday 4pm through Monday 8am here. We will take aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard and books. We ask that shredded paper be bagged. We also ask that your food and drink containers are rinsed clean. You can leave the caps on your items. https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2157
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center

item.title: 13th Annual Protective Services 5K - 04/18/2026 8:30am EDT to 04/18/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description:
Location : 491 Coe St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Join Us for the 13th Annual Protective Services 5K!

Get ready to lace up your sneakers and hit the pavement with fellow enthusiasts at this fun, in-person event to promote child abuse awareness and prevention. Whether you're a seasoned runner or prefer to walk, the 13th Annual Protective Services 5K is perfect for everyone. Bring your dog too! Come enjoy the energy, the community spirit, and show your support! Don’t miss out on the action—see you at the starting line! We will start at the American Legion Shelter at Hedges Boyer Park and follow the Rock Creek Trail.

We've experienced some sprinkles in years past and we don't let that stop us. In the event of significant rain, the event will be held at Frann's Fieldhouse, on the Heidelberg campus. Please watch our Facebook page (Seneca County JFS) for updates.

Dogs are welcome. Please have them on a leash and be sure to pick up anything they leave behind.

This is a free event.

8:30 am: Registration and packet pick-up

8:50 am: Opening Remarks

9: 00 am: Runners Start

9:05 am: Walkers Start
item.dc:creator: Seneca County JFS

item.title: Rick Springfield - 04/18/2026 7:30pm EDT to 04/18/2026 9:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Over the past four decades, Rick Springfield has worn many hats as an entertainer and performer. The creator of some of the finest power-pop of the ’80s, a Grammy winning singer, songwriter, and musician who has sold 25 million albums and scored 17 U.S. Top 40 hits, including “Jessie’s Girl,” “Don’t Talk to Strangers,” “An Affair of the Heart,” “I’ve Done Everything for You,” “Love Somebody,” and “Human Touch.” He’s an accomplished actor who has starred opposite Meryl Streep in the feature film “Ricki and the Flash,” gave a chameleonic performance as the creepy Dr. Irving Pitlor in HBO’s prestige drama “True Detective,” earned great reviews for his portrayal of Lucifer on the CW hit “Supernatural” and most recently played the insane Pastor Charles on FX’s worldwide hit series “American Horror Story.” An author as well, both his candid 2010 memoir Late, Late at Night (which Rolling Stone named one of the 25 greatest rock memoirs of all time) and his 2014 comedic novel Magnificent Vibration earned rave reviews and spots on the New York Times Best Sellers’ list. In 2014, Springfield was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, located around the corner from the first apartment he lived in when he first arrived in the U.S. from Australia in 1971. Springfield’s Sirius-XM radio show, Working Class DJ, is a huge hit and airs weekly on 80s on 8. His new album “Automatic” is out now. Automatic is his first album of all new original material in 5 years.

$125, $100, $75

Fan Club tickets go on sale November 10 at 10:00 AM

Public ticket sales begin November 17 at 10:00 AM.
item.dc:creator: The Ritz Theatre

item.title: Ribbon Cutting | Buckeye Community Schools - 04/20/2026 4:00pm EDT to 04/20/2026 5:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 870 W Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca Regional Chamber & Development Ambassadors in celebrating the grand opening of Buckeye Community Schools’ Tiffin location with a ribbon cutting and open house on April 20, 2026, from 4–6 PM. Buckeye Community Schools is a public charter high school serving students ages 14–21, offering an alternative educational environment designed to help students succeed on their own terms. With a unique paper-and-pencil curriculum and a personalized approach, each student is paired with the same instructor throughout their educational journey, building strong relationships, consistency, and support. BCS prides itself on creating a different kind of learning experience, meeting students where they are and empowering them to achieve their academic goals in a supportive and flexible setting. Guests are encouraged to stay for the open house, tour the new facility, and learn more about the impact Buckeye Community Schools is making in Seneca County.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: CASA Volunteer Training Orientation - 04/21/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/21/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 21 Court St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Imagine being a child, moving from home to home… Never knowing who to trust. Never having someone to speak up for you. Now imagine YOU being the person who changes that story. A steady voice. A safe place. A lifeline in a difficult time. Our Spring Volunteer Training Orientation is Tuesday, April 21 at 5:00 PM at our Tiffin office (21 Court Street). For about 8 hours a month, you can help a child look back on their childhood with gratitude instead of regret. No legal background required — just heart, time, and a commitment to stand by a child when they need it most. Learn more or apply at www.casaofssw.org or call 419-448-1442. Because right now, there are children who desperately need you.
item.dc:creator: CASA of SSW

Important Details:

APRIL 24-26, 2026

Friday 4p-8p, Saturday 8a-5p, & Sunday 8a-5p

item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA Join us for the Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend, held monthly on the 4th weekend from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM. This event takes place at various locations, including Wharton and Kirby, where you can drop off your recyclables. Accepted items include aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean; caps can remain on the items. For more details, visit our event calendar.

Come on your own, bring a friend, or build a group and join "Bunky’s Litter League", a community-wide initiative focused on litter cleanup and beautification. item.title: Day Of Giving For The Famous Aunt Ellen! - 04/26/2026 8:00am EDT to 04/26/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA If you know of Big Mike's, then you most definitely know The Famous Aunt Ellen. Words one could use to describe her are strong, faithful, funny, caring, kind, and selfless. Always been one to help someone else where I think accepting help is a hard thing for her to take. I am grateful that she has said yes to allowing us to put this event on for her to show her just how much we love her and want to help her in this battle! For anyone who does not know, Aunt Ellen was diagnosed with pancreatic cancer in the fall. She is currently doing chemotherapy and is responding really well to it thus far. We are hoping to raise money to help Aunt Ellen with medical bills during this difficult time. If you wish to donate, please reach out to me so I can add you on our list. We will be selling $10 raffle tickets for items to win with all the proceeds going right to The Famous Aunt Ellen! I know items already donated are a 2 hour ride party bike ride and 1 night stay at The Melmore Cabin! Many more cool prizes will be announced as they come in! We will be serving food all day long. We hope to have entertainment for kids and a band on the roof again! We will announce these options as we get them confirmed!
item.dc:creator: Big Mike's

item.title: Kate Quinn Author Visit - 04/28/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/28/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin-Seneca Public Library is excited to announce "The Astral Library" by Kate Quinn as the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read title. Each year the Community Read encourages people in Seneca County to read the selected title and come together for special events and discussions. As the featured event in the Community Read, Kate Quinn, a New York Times best-selling author, will speak to the community on Tuesday, April 28 at 6:30 p.m. at the Chandelier Community Event Center. The author event is completely free and open to the community and will be followed by a book signing. Copies of The Astral Library will be available for purchase thanks to the Pan Yan Bookstore both at the author event and at the Bookstore leading up to it. "The Astral Library" is “a gorgeously written fantastical adventure which poses the question: Have you ever wished you could live inside a book? Welcome to the Astral Library, where books are not just objects, but doors to new worlds, new lives, and new futures.” Just released in February, "The Astral Library" became an instant bestseller by reaching #2 in the New York Times Bestsellers list and #1 on the Globe & Mail bestsellers list in it’s first week of publication. Copies of the book will be available soon to be checked out from the Library, and the eBook and audiobook can be found on the Ohio Digital Library and Libby app. Additional events accompanying the author visit, including an adult craft night, book discussion, and more will be released in greater detail at a later date. The Christopher D. English Community Read was recently named in memory of former library trustee Christopher D. English and encourages Seneca County residents to read, think about, and discuss one outstanding title each spring, culminating in the author giving a public presentation in Tiffin. Past speakers have included New York Times best-selling authors Shelby Van Pelt, Kristin Harmel, Sarah Penner and Michael Hingson, Pulitzer Prize finalist and LA Times columnist Steve Lopez, and international best-selling author Janet Beard. The series is made possible through generous funding by the Tiffin-Seneca Library Foundation and the many donors who have contributed to the Foundation over the years. For more information call 419-447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: 24th Annual Good Samaritan Dinner & Auction - 04/29/2026 4:30pm EDT to 04/29/2026 5:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 631 Plaza Dr, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Dear Friends, We are pleased to announce the 24th Annual Good Shepherd Home Good Samaritan Dinner & Auction to be held on Wednesday, April 29, 2026. This year’s theme, “A Community in Bloom,” celebrates the compassion, generosity, and shared spirit that continue to strengthen our mission. The evening will once again be held at Stacy’s Place, 625 Plaza Drive, Fostoria. We warmly invite you to attend the dinner and/or contribute to the success of this special event, which helps raise funds for residents whose financial resources have been exhausted. As in previous years, all donors and sponsors will be recognized in our event program. Tickets are available for $40 per person and include appetizers, a seated dinner, dessert, and drinks. VIP tables are available for $400 and include seating for eight ( guests. In addition to donating auction items, we are excited to introduce our Blooming Sponsor Levels for those who wish to support the event through a monetary contribution: Violet Sponsor – $25 Rose Sponsor - $300 Daisy Sponsor – $75 Grand Garden Sponsor - $500 Tulip Sponsor – $150 Monetary sponsorships are gratefully accepted for those who wish to support the event in lieu of an item to contribute. To reserve tickets or inquire about sponsorship opportunities, please contact Crystal Macias at 419-937-1801 or cmacias@goodshepherdhome.com. If you would like to donate an auction item, please contact Wahneta Mockensturm at 419-937-1801 or wmockensturm@goodshepherdhome.com. Items may be dropped off, or we are happy to arrange pickup. On behalf of the Good Shepherd Home staff and the residents we serve, thank you for your continued generosity and support.
item.dc:creator: Good Shepherd Home

item.title: Encounter the Heart of Jesus: Adoration & Healing Prayer - 05/03/2026 6:30pm EDT to 05/03/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 323 N Wood St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA "'I will restore your health, I will heal your wounds,' says the Lord" - Jeremiah 30:17 God still desires to heal today! Encounter the Heart of Jesus evenings of Eucharistic Adoration will include communal prayer for healing both physical & spiritual wounds, as well as the opportunity for individual prayer ministry & the Sacrament of Confession, within the context of Eucharistic Adoration. St. Augustine said, "the Church is the traveler's inn where the wounded are healed." Please pray about who in your life (a friend, family member, coworker, neighbor) might be in need of the Lord's healing and invite them to come and encounter the Lord and experience His work in their lives. The Lord truly does desire to heal anything in our lives that keeps us from full freedom & life in Him! Come & see what the Lord wants to do in your life! ***Please note that December 7th & March 1st will be our parish's Advent/ Lent Penance Services in the context of Eucharistic Adoration and will NOT include prayer for healing.
item.dc:creator: St. Wendelin Parish

item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library's virtual craft series, "Make It At Home," and learn how to create a beautiful book page wreath starting Monday, May 4. Adults can pick up a free supply kit at the Information Desk, which includes most of the materials needed for the craft, requiring only a few basic supplies from home. An instructional video will be available on the library's YouTube channel and Facebook page. Kits are available on a first-come, first-served basis, limited to one per adult and must be collected in person. Registration is not required. If kits run out, you can still access the instructional video and information sheet at www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/maymakeitathome. Don't miss this creative opportunity!

item.title: How to Read a Book Like a Professor: Follow-Up Discussion - 05/07/2026 6:00pm EDT to 05/07/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the "How to Read a Book Like a Professor: Follow-Up Discussion" on May 7 at 6:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room at Tiffin-Seneca Public Library. This event is part of the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read, featuring the novel *The Astral Library* by Kate Quinn. Led by Heidelberg Professor Dr. Barry Devine, the discussion will reflect on the novel and revisit Dr. Devine's earlier session on reading techniques to enhance the experience. The Community Read invites the Seneca County community to engage with the selected title through special events, including a free author talk by New York Times best-selling author Kate Quinn on April 28 at 6:30 p.m. at the Chandelier Community Event Center, followed by a book signing. Copies of *The Astral Library* will be available for purchase at the event. For more information about the Community Read and additional programs, visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Nonprofit Leadership Alliance | Healthy Teams, Strong Missions - 05/08/2026 11:00am EDT to 05/08/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 928 W Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca Regional Chamber & Development and United Way of North Central Ohio for the second Nonprofit Leadership Alliance quarterly meeting of 2026 on Friday, May 8 at 11 AM, hosted at North Central Ohio ESC. This session is designed for nonprofit leaders, staff, and board members who want to better support themselves and their teams while sustaining meaningful, mission-driven work. The featured topic, “Healthy Teams, Strong Missions: Preventing Burnout & Supporting Mental Well-Being,” will focus on creating workplace cultures that recognize stress, prioritize well-being, and prevent burnout. Presenters Erin Perry, MA, LPCC-S, owner of Counseling Connections Center, and Kim Radison, owner of KKR Consult, will share practical strategies nonprofit organizations can use to promote mental health, build resilience, and support staff at all levels. Attendees will learn how to recognize common signs of stress and burnout, apply self-care and resilience practices, and foster healthier, more supportive work environments. This session is designed to offer realistic, actionable tools that can be implemented immediately within nonprofit teams. Following the presentation, Erin Perry and Kim Radison will offer attendees the opportunity to schedule a free one-on-one session for additional personalized support or consultation. These meetings are free and open to all nonprofit employees and board members in Seneca County. Chamber & Development membership is not required. Join us for an important conversation about caring for the people behind the mission. Register today to secure your spot.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: Spring Migration Bird Hike - 05/09/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/09/2026 10:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 1143 Township Rd 243, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Join us for the Spring Migration Bird Hike at Aeraland Recreation Area! This guided hike is a wonderful opportunity to observe many interesting and colorful birds, including Eastern Meadowlark, Orchard Oriole, Indigo Bunting, Red-eyed Vireo, and a variety of vibrant warblers as they journey to their northern breeding grounds. Binoculars will be provided, or you can bring your own. The event starts at 9 AM, is open to all ages, and is free with no registration required. For more information, you can contact Tim Kleman at tkleman@hancockparks.com.

item.title: "Still Life Arrangement & Painting" Mondays, April 6th and 13th, 6pm-9pm #04062602 - 04/06/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/13/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by March 30th. Paint your own still life with your favorite item and fruit or flowers. You will paint in water-mixable oils with Carol Hoffman on two Mondays April 6th and 13th from 6pm-9pm. These are 3 hour classes. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the canvas are included in the price of the class. Cost is $70 for TAG members & $80 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419-547-8610, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: "Barn on the Hill" Thursday, April 9, 2026 10am-1pm or 6pm-9pm #04092601 or 04092602 - 04/09/2026 10:00am EDT to 04/09/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by April 9. Paint "Barn on Hill" in water-mixable oils with Carol Hoffman on Thursday, April 9 from 10am until 1 pm or from 6pm-9pm. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the 9"x12" canvas are included in the price of the class. Please bring a large box to take your painting home in. Cost is $50 for TAG members & $60 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419-547-8610, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: Seneca County: A Land of Immigrants - 04/09/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/09/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin-Seneca Public Library invites you to "Seneca County: A Land of Immigrants" on Thursday, April 9 at 6:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Join Local History and Genealogy Specialist Jessica Hoehn for an engaging exploration of the rich cultural heritage of Seneca County. This program will delve into the stories of various immigrant groups who have settled in the area, highlighting their challenges and the lasting impact on community traditions, industries, and neighborhoods. The event is free and open to all adults and is part of T-SPL’s America 250 celebration. For more information, call (419) 447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Game Bird Workshop - 04/09/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/09/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 100 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Are you interested in raising pheasants, quail, or other game birds for sport, meat production, or conservation? This comprehensive workshop will provide you with the essential knowledge and practical skills needed to start and manage your own healthy flock. Whether you're a small-scale farmer, a future hunting preserve owner, or a hobbyist, join us to learn the best practices for successful game bird husbandry. This workshop is free but registration is required. Dinner will be provided starting at 5:30pm.
item.dc:creator: Ohio State University Extension - Seneca County

item.title: Downtown Trivia Night at the Tiffin Eagles - 04/09/2026 7:00pm EDT to 04/09/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Groups of all ages are invited to compete in two rounds of general trivia questions plus the final questions of local interest. Answers will be judged by local celebrities. Participants are asked to form teams of 6 or fewer, which can be composed of all ages. Teams will write their answers to be judged by local celebrities. Questions will be geared toward ages 12 and up. No phones, tablets, or electronic devices may be accessed during the contest. A traveling trophy will be awarded to the winning team. **ONE GrowthZone ticket per team. List each of the team members on the ticket.

item.title: Marseilles Recycling Drop Off collection weekend - 04/11/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/11/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA You can drop off your recyclables every month on the 2nd weekend from Friday 4pm through Monday 8am here. We will take aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard and books. We ask that shredded paper be bagged. We also ask that your food and drink containers are rinsed clean. You can leave the caps on your items. https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2417
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center

item.title: "White Trees" Saturday, April 11, 2026 9am-1pm #04112601 - 04/11/2026 9:00am EDT to 04/11/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by April 4th. Paint "White Trees" in acrylic with Carol Hoffman on Saturday, April 11 from 9 am until 1 pm. This is a 4 hour class. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the 12x16" canvas are included in the price of the class. Please bring a large box to take your painting home in. Cost is $55 for TAG members & $65 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419-547-8610, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.

item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA This is the 4th year for this event! We will have vendors and a kick butt silent auction! And don't forget POUND! What is POUND? Cardio drumming on steroids! BUT, it is for everybody and every BODY. From 4 years old to 100 years old, everyone can do it!! Buy a shirt, join in the Pound, check out the vendors and food trucks. item.title: 2026 Downtown Tiffin Chocolate Walk - 04/11/2026 11:00am EDT to 04/11/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 112 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Downtown Tiffin Chocolate Walk Treat yourself to a sweet afternoon in downtown Tiffin at the Downtown Tiffin Chocolate Walk on Saturday, April 11, 2026 11:00AM-3:00PM Stroll from business to business and enjoy chocolate treats and samples at 20 participating downtown locations. This self-guided walk is a fun way to shop local, explore downtown, and enjoy a spring day filled with sweet stops along the way. Date: Saturday, April 11, 2026 Time: 11:00AM-3:00PM Location: Downtown Tiffin Tickets: $20 Tickets are limited and must be purchased in advance at: https://tinyurl.com/3aurxrm9 Gather your friends and enjoy a delicious stroll through Downtown Tiffin. item.title: Poker Night at the Fire Station! - 04/11/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/12/2026 12:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 120 Catherine St, Green Springs, OH 44836, USA Saturday Nov 8th Proceeds benefit the Independence day celebration! Doors open: 5pm cards play: 6pm No late entries! $30 buy in unlimited buy backs for first 3 rounds $10 buy-up for after 3rd round blinds double every 30 minutes bring a new player, you and them get an additional $100 in chips Starting stack $1k beer, pop, food will be available for purchase Pre-register and pay by 11/5 for an additional $100 in chips no late registrations after 6pm must be 18 call/ text 419-307-3675 to register Paypal and Square app Available for online payment through the fire department accounts. Only pay through posts from our page and NO OTHERS!

item.title: Night of Worship with Rekindle - 04/12/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/12/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 8245 Main St, Old Fort, OH 44861, USA Sunday, April 12, at 6:30 pm, Old Fort Church will host the group Rekindle from Cedarville University for a Night of Worship. This event will be FREE and open to the public. Rekindle is an all-female worship ministry led by young ladies studying worship and they serve the body of Christ primarily at regional women's events, in local churches and on international mission trips. Rekindle represents the worship program of the Department of Music and Worship at Cedarville University. Rekindle's worship services are scripture based, musically excellent, and infused with congregational songs for the Church. Rekindle is a verb meaning "relight (a fire)" or to "revive (something that has been lost)." Just as David cried out for God to "revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you (Psalm 85:6)," so too Rekindle seeks to lead the people of God to cry out for that revival spirit to return to the Church.
item.dc:creator: Old Fort Church

item.title: Seneca County Human Resources Association | April Meeting - 04/14/2026 11:45am EDT to 04/14/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 45 St Lawrence Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca County Human Resources Association for our April meeting on Tuesday, April 14, 2026, from 11:45 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. in the McMullen Conference Room at Mercy Health – Tiffin Hospital. We will be joined by Renee Smith and Sarah Stephens Krupp of Thrive Community Partners. In this session, Thrive Community Partners will share real examples from local employers who identified specific, on-demand needs and how targeted training helped address them. Participants will walk through a simple “on-demand training loop” they can adapt inside their own organizations to quickly respond to emerging workforce challenges. The Seneca County Human Resources Association (SCHRA) connects HR professionals, business leaders, and organizational managers across Seneca County to share best practices, discuss workforce trends, and grow professionally. Meetings focus on practical learning, peer discussion, and updates on programs and resources that support hiring, engagement, compliance, and organizational success. Membership in the Seneca County Human Resources Association (SCHRA) is open to anyone with HR responsibilities or an interest in workforce and personnel development. SCHRA operates on a one-time yearly registration, allowing members to sign up once for the entire year rather than registering for each individual meeting. Registered members receive meeting notices, speaker details, and calendar invitations for every upcoming session throughout the year. To register for SCHRA visit: https://www.developsenecacounty.org/schra-interest-form/
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: T-SPL Talks: Treatment Foster Care - 04/14/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/14/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join Tiffin-Seneca Public Library on Tuesday, April 14 at 6:00 p.m. for "T-SPL Talks: Treatment Foster Care," part of the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read. This event, held in the Frost Kalnow Room, features a presentation from Northwest Ohio Treatment Foster Care, one of this year's Community Impact Partners. Attendees will learn about treatment foster care, its role in supporting children with higher needs, the requirements to become a foster caregiver, and how the community can get involved. The Community Read centers around the book "The Astral Library" by Kate Quinn, promoting unity through reading and discussions. In addition, best-selling author Kate Quinn will speak at a free community event on Tuesday, April 28 at 6:30 p.m. at the Chandelier Community Event Center, followed by a book signing. For more information about the Community Read and upcoming programs, visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.

item.title: Freedom Rising XII - 04/16/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/16/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA ANNOUNCEMENT: FREEDOM RISING XII We are thrilled to officially announce Freedom Rising XII, our annual celebration and largest fundraiser of the year benefiting Sisters in Shelter and it’s an evening you won’t want to miss. This powerful night brings our community together in support of survivors of human trafficking through an unforgettable program that includes: Inspiring program & mission updates Raffle & Silent Auction (with over 100 incredible items last year!) Heavy hors d’oeuvres Cash bar Keynote Speaker: Brittany Joy Figlar Brittany’s story is one of courage, resilience, and transformation. Each time she speaks, she moves hearts and challenges us to see what’s possible when survivors are empowered to rise from victim to victor. Get Involved: • Interested in donating a raffle or silent auction item or basket? We would be deeply grateful for your support. • Sponsorship opportunities are available at multiple levels, offering meaningful ways to stand with survivors while showing your commitment to our mission. $1,000 Gold Sponsorship includes: 1 VIP Table (8 seats) 8 Tickets to event 8 Free drink tickets 2 Sisters in Shelter T-Shirts Swag bag Advertising on Social Media, Newspaper, Printed Materials, and Radio! $500 Silver Sponsorship includes: 4 Tickets to event 4 Free drink tickets Swag bag Advertising on Social Media, Newspaper, Printed Materials, and Radio! • And even if sponsorship isn’t the right fit, every donation, at any amount, makes a difference. We truly could not do this without our community. We are so blessed to live in a place that shows up for local businesses, for organizations, and most importantly… for people. Tickets are now available (https://www.zeffy.com/en-US/ticketing/freedom-rising-xii ) join us for a night of purpose, impact, and hope. This is more than an event. It’s a movement. We look forward to connecting with you: Sisters in Shelter Any Questions or Contact information: 201 S. Washington Street, PO Box 384 Tiffin, Ohio 44883 (567) 205-8771 sistersinshelter@gmail.com
item.dc:creator: Sisters in Shelter

item.title: Freedom Rising XII Celebration and Fundraiser - 04/16/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/16/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Join community and business leaders for an unforgettable evening with Sisters in Shelter at their Freedom Rising XII, 5:00-7:30 pm Thursday April 16, 2026, at The Chandelier Community Event Center, 151 S. Washington Street, Tiffin.

 

The night will include a keynote presentation from Brittany Joy Figlar who brings a voice to the stage that is both courageous and unforgettable. A survivor of human trafficking, Brittany has transformed her lived experience into powerful advocacy—educating communities, training professionals, and challenging systems to better protect and support survivors. Her story is raw, honest, and deeply moving, reminding us not only of the realities of exploitation, but of the strength and resilience it takes to rise beyond it. Hearing Brittany speak is not just inspiring, it’s a moment that stays with you long after the night ends.

 

Your support at this event will directly contribute to critical support services provided for survivors which include shelter, counseling, legal aid, and vocational training; and to expand awareness campaigns by educating community members to recognize the signs of human trafficking and how to report it.

Tickets for the Freedom Rising XII Celebration and Fundraiser are available now.  There will be a cash bar & heavy hors d'oeuvres.  There will be 50/50, raffles, and silent auction with all proceeds to benefit Sisters in Shelter. Business attire is appropriate. Don't miss this opportunity to be a part of something truly impactful. Together we can make a difference.

 

Sisters in Shelter, which began in 2005, is a non-profit organization that supports survivors and illuminate the atrocities of human trafficking through community education and advocacy. Sisters in Shelter provides critical first needs for women survivors of human trafficking: emergency safe housing, medical care, safety planning, and legal advocacy. To donate directly to Sisters in Shelter, or to learn about volunteer opportunities, visit sistersinshelter.com or visit Facebook: Sisters in Shelter.
item.dc:creator: Seneca County Commission on Aging and Sisters in Shelter

item.title: "The Abstract Show" will be displayed from April 1 -25. Intake will be from March 25-28. - 04/16/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 8:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Get ready for a bold, vibrant, and unpredictable art experience! The Abstract Art Exhibit is all about letting go, embracing creativity, and seeing the world through an entirely new lens. Whether you're an artist or an admirer, this is a show that will challenge, inspire, and excite both the creator and the viewer! Abstract art is more than just shapes and colors, it's about emotion, movement, and limitless expression. Every piece in this exhibit pushes the boundaries of imagination, drawing you into a world where rules are meant to be broken, and where creativity knows no limits. Expect a dazzling mix of mediums, including photography, painting, sculpture, textiles, digital art, jewelry, pastels, glass, and more. If you can dream it, it belongs here! Whether youÕre showcasing your work or simply soaking in the creativity, donÕt miss this one-of-a kind exhibit. Step inside, explore, and let your imagination run wild!
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: Into the Woods - 04/16/2026 7:00pm EDT to 04/16/2026 9:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : University Hall, 310 E Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre presents Into the Woods.

Be careful what you wish for... 🌲✨

Heidelberg University School of Music and Theatre invites you to a spellbinding production of Stephen Sondheim and James Lapine’s Into the Woods. Watch as the worlds of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, and Jack and the Beanstalk collide in a darkly comic exploration of what happens after "happily ever after."

Join a baker and his wife on their quest to break a witch's curse—and discover the true cost of getting everything you ever wanted.

📍 Where: Gundlach Theatre 

📅 When: * April 16, 17, & 18 | 7:00 PM

April 19 | 2:00 PM

Tickets:

 https://www.eventbrite.com/cc/into-the-woods-4810285

Poster Design by Bri Clark, BA’23 and MBA’24

Into the Woods is presented through special arrangement with Music Theatre International (MTI).

 All authorized performance materials are also supplied by MTI.

item.title: Cold Case Analysis and Community Impact: Inside TU’s Fellowship Program - 04/17/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/17/2026 1:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 155 Miami St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The upcoming event features a two-part discussion focusing on significant historical and social themes. The first segment, led by Dr. Michael Curtis, the Director of Tiffin University's Cold Case Fellowship, will analyze the impact of cold case investigations on communities, highlighting the experiences of students involved in the fellowship program. The second segment will be conducted by Dr. Susan Trollinger, a Professor of English and Rhetoric at the University of Dayton, who will explore the embodied rhetoric employed by women during the suffrage movement, examining the ways in which their attire and presentation influenced public perception and mobilization for women's rights. Together, these presentations will offer insights into the intersection of academic inquiry, community engagement, and historical advocacy.
item.dc:creator: LIFE@TU

item.title: Playtime at the Library - 04/17/2026 10:00am EDT to 04/17/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Youth Services Department at the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library will be holding “Playtime @ the Library” on Fridays, April 3 and 17 from 10:00 a.m. to noon in the Frost Kalnow Room. Hosted with Seneca County’s Ohio Early Intervention, toys, and activities will be offered to children ages 18 months to five years, to interact with their caregivers in an unstructured, creative play. item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA You can drop off your recyclables every month on the 3rd weekend of the month from Friday 4pm through Monday 8am here. We will take aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard and books. We ask that shredded paper be bagged. item.title: 13th Annual Protective Services 5K - 04/18/2026 8:30am EDT to 04/18/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description:
Location : 491 Coe St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Join Us for the 13th Annual Protective Services 5K!

Get ready to lace up your sneakers and hit the pavement with fellow enthusiasts at this fun, in-person event to promote child abuse awareness and prevention. Whether you're a seasoned runner or prefer to walk, the 13th Annual Protective Services 5K is perfect for everyone. Bring your dog too! Come enjoy the energy, the community spirit, and show your support! Don’t miss out on the action—see you at the starting line! We will start at the American Legion Shelter at Hedges Boyer Park and follow the Rock Creek Trail.

We've experienced some sprinkles in years past and we don't let that stop us. In the event of significant rain, the event will be held at Frann's Fieldhouse, on the Heidelberg campus. Please watch our Facebook page (Seneca County JFS) for updates.

Dogs are welcome. Please have them on a leash and be sure to pick up anything they leave behind.

This is a free event.

8:30 am: Registration and packet pick-up

8:50 am: Opening Remarks

9: 00 am: Runners Start

9:05 am: Walkers Start
item.dc:creator: Seneca County JFS

item.title: Rick Springfield - 04/18/2026 7:30pm EDT to 04/18/2026 9:30pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Over the past four decades, Rick Springfield has worn many hats as an entertainer and performer. The creator of some of the finest power-pop of the ’80s, a Grammy winning singer, songwriter, and musician who has sold 25 million albums and scored 17 U.S. Top 40 hits, including “Jessie’s Girl,” “Don’t Talk to Strangers,” “An Affair of the Heart,” “I’ve Done Everything for You,” “Love Somebody,” and “Human Touch.” He’s an accomplished actor who has starred opposite Meryl Streep in the feature film “Ricki and the Flash,” gave a chameleonic performance as the creepy Dr. Irving Pitlor in HBO’s prestige drama “True Detective,” earned great reviews for his portrayal of Lucifer on the CW hit “Supernatural” and most recently played the insane Pastor Charles on FX’s worldwide hit series “American Horror Story.” An author as well, both his candid 2010 memoir Late, Late at Night (which Rolling Stone named one of the 25 greatest rock memoirs of all time) and his 2014 comedic novel Magnificent Vibration earned rave reviews and spots on the New York Times Best Sellers’ list. In 2014, Springfield was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, located around the corner from the first apartment he lived in when he first arrived in the U.S. from Australia in 1971. Springfield’s Sirius-XM radio show, Working Class DJ, is a huge hit and airs weekly on 80s on 8. His new album “Automatic” is out now. Automatic is his first album of all new original material in 5 years.

$125, $100, $75

Fan Club tickets go on sale November 10 at 10:00 AM

Public ticket sales begin November 17 at 10:00 AM.
item.dc:creator: The Ritz Theatre

item.title: Ribbon Cutting | Buckeye Community Schools - 04/20/2026 4:00pm EDT to 04/20/2026 5:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 870 W Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca Regional Chamber & Development Ambassadors in celebrating the grand opening of Buckeye Community Schools’ Tiffin location with a ribbon cutting and open house on April 20, 2026, from 4–6 PM. Buckeye Community Schools is a public charter high school serving students ages 14–21, offering an alternative educational environment designed to help students succeed on their own terms. With a unique paper-and-pencil curriculum and a personalized approach, each student is paired with the same instructor throughout their educational journey, building strong relationships, consistency, and support. BCS prides itself on creating a different kind of learning experience, meeting students where they are and empowering them to achieve their academic goals in a supportive and flexible setting. Guests are encouraged to stay for the open house, tour the new facility, and learn more about the impact Buckeye Community Schools is making in Seneca County.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: CASA Volunteer Training Orientation - 04/21/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/21/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 21 Court St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Imagine being a child, moving from home to home… Never knowing who to trust. Never having someone to speak up for you. Now imagine YOU being the person who changes that story. A steady voice. A safe place. A lifeline in a difficult time. Our Spring Volunteer Training Orientation is Tuesday, April 21 at 5:00 PM at our Tiffin office (21 Court Street). For about 8 hours a month, you can help a child look back on their childhood with gratitude instead of regret. No legal background required — just heart, time, and a commitment to stand by a child when they need it most. Learn more or apply at www.casaofssw.org or call 419-448-1442. Because right now, there are children who desperately need you.
item.dc:creator: CASA of SSW

item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA Join us for the Recycling Drop Off Collection Weekend, held monthly on the 4th weekend from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM. This event takes place at various locations, including Wharton and Kirby, where you can drop off your recyclables. Accepted items include aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean; caps can remain on the items. For more details, visit our event calendar.

item.title: Day Of Giving For The Famous Aunt Ellen! - 04/26/2026 8:00am EDT to 04/26/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA If you know of Big Mike's, then you most definitely know The Famous Aunt Ellen. Words one could use to describe her are strong, faithful, funny, caring, kind, and selfless. Always been one to help someone else where I think accepting help is a hard thing for her to take. I am grateful that she has said yes to allowing us to put this event on for her to show her just how much we love her and want to help her in this battle! For anyone who does not know, Aunt Ellen was diagnosed with pancreatic cancer in the fall. She is currently doing chemotherapy and is responding really well to it thus far. We are hoping to raise money to help Aunt Ellen with medical bills during this difficult time. If you wish to donate, please reach out to me so I can add you on our list. We will be selling $10 raffle tickets for items to win with all the proceeds going right to The Famous Aunt Ellen! I know items already donated are a 2 hour ride party bike ride and 1 night stay at The Melmore Cabin! Many more cool prizes will be announced as they come in! We will be serving food all day long. We hope to have entertainment for kids and a band on the roof again! We will announce these options as we get them confirmed!
item.dc:creator: Big Mike's

item.title: Kate Quinn Author Visit - 04/28/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/28/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin-Seneca Public Library is excited to announce "The Astral Library" by Kate Quinn as the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read title. Each year the Community Read encourages people in Seneca County to read the selected title and come together for special events and discussions. As the featured event in the Community Read, Kate Quinn, a New York Times best-selling author, will speak to the community on Tuesday, April 28 at 6:30 p.m. at the Chandelier Community Event Center. The author event is completely free and open to the community and will be followed by a book signing. Copies of The Astral Library will be available for purchase thanks to the Pan Yan Bookstore both at the author event and at the Bookstore leading up to it. "The Astral Library" is “a gorgeously written fantastical adventure which poses the question: Have you ever wished you could live inside a book? Welcome to the Astral Library, where books are not just objects, but doors to new worlds, new lives, and new futures.” Just released in February, "The Astral Library" became an instant bestseller by reaching #2 in the New York Times Bestsellers list and #1 on the Globe & Mail bestsellers list in it’s first week of publication. Copies of the book will be available soon to be checked out from the Library, and the eBook and audiobook can be found on the Ohio Digital Library and Libby app. Additional events accompanying the author visit, including an adult craft night, book discussion, and more will be released in greater detail at a later date. The Christopher D. English Community Read was recently named in memory of former library trustee Christopher D. English and encourages Seneca County residents to read, think about, and discuss one outstanding title each spring, culminating in the author giving a public presentation in Tiffin. Past speakers have included New York Times best-selling authors Shelby Van Pelt, Kristin Harmel, Sarah Penner and Michael Hingson, Pulitzer Prize finalist and LA Times columnist Steve Lopez, and international best-selling author Janet Beard. The series is made possible through generous funding by the Tiffin-Seneca Library Foundation and the many donors who have contributed to the Foundation over the years. For more information call 419-447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: 24th Annual Good Samaritan Dinner & Auction - 04/29/2026 4:30pm EDT to 04/29/2026 5:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 631 Plaza Dr, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Dear Friends, We are pleased to announce the 24th Annual Good Shepherd Home Good Samaritan Dinner & Auction to be held on Wednesday, April 29, 2026. This year’s theme, “A Community in Bloom,” celebrates the compassion, generosity, and shared spirit that continue to strengthen our mission. The evening will once again be held at Stacy’s Place, 625 Plaza Drive, Fostoria. We warmly invite you to attend the dinner and/or contribute to the success of this special event, which helps raise funds for residents whose financial resources have been exhausted. As in previous years, all donors and sponsors will be recognized in our event program. Tickets are available for $40 per person and include appetizers, a seated dinner, dessert, and drinks. VIP tables are available for $400 and include seating for eight ( guests. In addition to donating auction items, we are excited to introduce our Blooming Sponsor Levels for those who wish to support the event through a monetary contribution: Violet Sponsor – $25 Rose Sponsor - $300 Daisy Sponsor – $75 Grand Garden Sponsor - $500 Tulip Sponsor – $150 Monetary sponsorships are gratefully accepted for those who wish to support the event in lieu of an item to contribute. To reserve tickets or inquire about sponsorship opportunities, please contact Crystal Macias at 419-937-1801 or cmacias@goodshepherdhome.com. If you would like to donate an auction item, please contact Wahneta Mockensturm at 419-937-1801 or wmockensturm@goodshepherdhome.com. Items may be dropped off, or we are happy to arrange pickup. On behalf of the Good Shepherd Home staff and the residents we serve, thank you for your continued generosity and support.
item.dc:creator: Good Shepherd Home

item.description: Location : 8919 OH-53, McCutchenville, OH 44844, USA Join us for the monthly Recycling Drop-Off collection weekends in McCutchenville and Harpster, running from Friday at 4 PM to Monday at 8 AM on the first weekend of every month. You can recycle aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard, and books. Please ensure that shredded paper is bagged and that food and drink containers are rinsed clean, although you may leave the caps on your items. For more details, visit the Wyandot County calendar at the links provided: [McCutchenville](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2366) and [Harpster](https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2314).

item.title: Encounter the Heart of Jesus: Adoration & Healing Prayer - 05/03/2026 6:30pm EDT to 05/03/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 323 N Wood St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA "'I will restore your health, I will heal your wounds,' says the Lord" - Jeremiah 30:17 God still desires to heal today! Encounter the Heart of Jesus evenings of Eucharistic Adoration will include communal prayer for healing both physical & spiritual wounds, as well as the opportunity for individual prayer ministry & the Sacrament of Confession, within the context of Eucharistic Adoration. St. Augustine said, "the Church is the traveler's inn where the wounded are healed." Please pray about who in your life (a friend, family member, coworker, neighbor) might be in need of the Lord's healing and invite them to come and encounter the Lord and experience His work in their lives. The Lord truly does desire to heal anything in our lives that keeps us from full freedom & life in Him! Come & see what the Lord wants to do in your life! ***Please note that December 7th & March 1st will be our parish's Advent/ Lent Penance Services in the context of Eucharistic Adoration and will NOT include prayer for healing.
item.dc:creator: St. Wendelin Parish

item.title: Make it at Home: Book Page Wreath - 05/04/2026 9:30am EDT to 05/04/2026 10:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library's virtual craft series, "Make It At Home," and learn how to create a beautiful book page wreath starting Monday, May 4. Adults can pick up a free supply kit at the Information Desk, which includes most of the materials needed for the craft, requiring only a few basic supplies from home. An instructional video will be available on the library's YouTube channel and Facebook page. Kits are available on a first-come, first-served basis, limited to one per adult and must be collected in person. Registration is not required. If kits run out, you can still access the instructional video and information sheet at www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/maymakeitathome. Don't miss this creative opportunity!

item.title: How to Read a Book Like a Professor: Follow-Up Discussion - 05/07/2026 6:00pm EDT to 05/07/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the "How to Read a Book Like a Professor: Follow-Up Discussion" on May 7 at 6:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room at Tiffin-Seneca Public Library. This event is part of the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read, featuring the novel *The Astral Library* by Kate Quinn. Led by Heidelberg Professor Dr. Barry Devine, the discussion will reflect on the novel and revisit Dr. Devine's earlier session on reading techniques to enhance the experience. The Community Read invites the Seneca County community to engage with the selected title through special events, including a free author talk by New York Times best-selling author Kate Quinn on April 28 at 6:30 p.m. at the Chandelier Community Event Center, followed by a book signing. Copies of *The Astral Library* will be available for purchase at the event. For more information about the Community Read and additional programs, visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Nonprofit Leadership Alliance | Healthy Teams, Strong Missions - 05/08/2026 11:00am EDT to 05/08/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 928 W Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca Regional Chamber & Development and United Way of North Central Ohio for the second Nonprofit Leadership Alliance quarterly meeting of 2026 on Friday, May 8 at 11 AM, hosted at North Central Ohio ESC. This session is designed for nonprofit leaders, staff, and board members who want to better support themselves and their teams while sustaining meaningful, mission-driven work. The featured topic, “Healthy Teams, Strong Missions: Preventing Burnout & Supporting Mental Well-Being,” will focus on creating workplace cultures that recognize stress, prioritize well-being, and prevent burnout. Presenters Erin Perry, MA, LPCC-S, owner of Counseling Connections Center, and Kim Radison, owner of KKR Consult, will share practical strategies nonprofit organizations can use to promote mental health, build resilience, and support staff at all levels. Attendees will learn how to recognize common signs of stress and burnout, apply self-care and resilience practices, and foster healthier, more supportive work environments. This session is designed to offer realistic, actionable tools that can be implemented immediately within nonprofit teams. Following the presentation, Erin Perry and Kim Radison will offer attendees the opportunity to schedule a free one-on-one session for additional personalized support or consultation. These meetings are free and open to all nonprofit employees and board members in Seneca County. Chamber & Development membership is not required. Join us for an important conversation about caring for the people behind the mission. Register today to secure your spot.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: Spring Migration Bird Hike - 05/09/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/09/2026 10:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 1143 Township Rd 243, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Join us for the Spring Migration Bird Hike at Aeraland Recreation Area! This guided hike is a wonderful opportunity to observe many interesting and colorful birds, including Eastern Meadowlark, Orchard Oriole, Indigo Bunting, Red-eyed Vireo, and a variety of vibrant warblers as they journey to their northern breeding grounds. Binoculars will be provided, or you can bring your own. The event starts at 9 AM, is open to all ages, and is free with no registration required. For more information, you can contact Tim Kleman at tkleman@hancockparks.com.
item.dc:creator: Hancock Park District

item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by May 2nd. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the 12x16" canvas are included in the price of the class. Please bring a large box to take your painting home in. Cost is $55 for TAG members & $65 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419-547-8610, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.

item.title: Puzzle Swap - 05/09/2026 10:00am EDT to 05/09/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the Puzzle Swap at the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library on Saturday, May 9, from 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. This event allows you to give your beloved puzzles a second life by either donating them or selecting a new one to take home. Feel free to participate without needing to contribute a puzzle, and don’t forget to ensure that any donated puzzles are complete. This free program is open to all adults. For more details, contact the library at 419-447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library
